source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shared shell freshness and recovery vocabulary
source_refs.rationale: Shared stale/recovery vocabulary is authoritative.
Cache legality envelope is authoritative.

rows.stale_view_posture: Rows remain mounted while basis drift rebases inline and mutation affordances fail closed.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the originating inbox row or the narrowest surviving filter chip.
rows.artifact_posture: Queue rows summarize current state only; attachments stay subordinate until workspace entry.
step_up_checkpoints: Privileged reassignment or escalation may trigger inline step-up under northbound command law.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Refresh and reconnect preserve filters, row order, and the anchored row.
Notification-open returns to the same queue row when the object remains legal.
No full-page reset for ordinary row updates.

source_refs.rationale: The route and queue semantics are explicit.
Queue updates, escalation posture, and stale protection are explicitly testable.

rows.stale_view_posture: Keep the full calm-shell frame mounted and downgrade unsafe mutations to `NO_SAFE_ACTION` during rebase.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the originating module tab, composer trigger, or queue row anchor.
rows.artifact_posture: Current artifacts, linked context, and history remain visibly distinct inside the same shell.
step_up_checkpoints: Risky status changes and authority-dependent actions stay inline and shell-local.
recovery_and_resume_rules: The ordered reading path remains `CONTEXT_BAR -> DECISION_SUMMARY -> ACTION_STRIP -> DETAIL_DRAWER`.
Rebase preserves the same item shell, draft context, and selected module when still legal.
Focus returns to the route-local anchor after module, drawer, or composer transitions.

source_refs.rationale: The four-surface order and module grammar are explicit.
The calm-shell ordered reading path is shared law.

rows.stale_view_posture: Preserve the thread and composer while invalidating only the unsafe submission path.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the customer-activity tab or the exact composer trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Only customer-visible activity and customer-safe attachment posture may appear here.
step_up_checkpoints: Sensitive outbound requests stay bound to the work-item stale-view guard.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Customer-visible concurrency is visibility-scoped.
Inline refresh preserves scroll position and composer context when legal.

source_refs.rationale: Customer-visible activity is separately stored and streamed.
The customer thread read surface and command surface are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Thread stays mounted but the note composer fails closed if the workspace basis drifts.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the internal-activity tab, latest note anchor, or the invoking queue row.
rows.artifact_posture: Internal-only notes and attachments never appear in customer-facing routes.
step_up_checkpoints: Higher-privilege note entry stays policy-gated and non-leaky.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Internal-only activity does not stale or notify customer-visible routes.
Append-only history remains visible during reconnect and rebase.

source_refs.rationale: Internal-only activity must never appear in customer-visible surfaces.
The customer-visible omission and stale behavior are explicit regression scenarios.

rows.stale_view_posture: Artifact rows stay visible as stale context but cannot be promoted to current on a drifted view.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the exact artifact row or preview launcher that opened detail.
rows.artifact_posture: Current-vs-history posture is explicit and quarantined files never masquerade as downloadable current artifacts.
step_up_checkpoints: Sensitive download or export posture remains policy-governed and may require step-up.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Current and historical artifact anchors survive inline refresh.
Quarantined or replacement-required files remain visible as typed recovery context.

source_refs.rationale: The files module is a first-class workspace component.
Current-vs-history and quarantined download posture are explicit scenarios.

rows.stale_view_posture: The manifest route remains mounted; linked workflow focus rebases inline or drops back to the manifest anchor.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the manifest-local workflow focus anchor or the parent manifest navigator.
rows.artifact_posture: Manifest truth remains primary while workflow context opens as linked support detail.
step_up_checkpoints: Manifest-local filing or approval step-up still stays route-local to the manifest shell.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Same object, same shell applies to the manifest route even when workflow focus is active.
The focus query must restore the linked work-item anchor after refresh if still legal.

source_refs.rationale: The explicit manifest focus pattern is named here.
Focus jumps cannot invent a new shell identity.

rows.stale_view_posture: The list refreshes in place and never shows internal-only fields even during reconnect.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the originating request row or list filter.
rows.artifact_posture: Only current safe request state and customer-safe history summaries may appear.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Opening and closing request detail preserves the parent request-row anchor.
Cross-device continuity restores the same request when still legal.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, support budget, and constrained layouts
source_refs.rationale: Client-shell continuity and return-target rules apply.

rows.stale_view_posture: The request card stays mounted with inline recovery and explicit no-safe-action language when needed.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the request focus anchor, current artifact row, or the invoking request list row.
rows.artifact_posture: Current artifact, history, and support handoff stay explicit and customer-safe.
step_up_checkpoints: Identity or authority verification stays inside the route rather than redirecting to a new shell.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Return from browser or authority handoff never implies completion until the request projection settles.
Historical artifacts remain visible as context but never become the default current artifact on rebase.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.
Customer-safe return, handoff, and artifact posture rules are shared.

rows.stale_view_posture: The hero and task queue remain visible while stale or reconnect posture is explained inline.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the status hero primary action or the exact task queue row that launched a deeper route.
rows.artifact_posture: Home uses summary-first current-state cards; artifacts stay subordinate to task selection.
step_up_checkpoints: Home may route into in-place step-up, but does not force a new top-level destination.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Home must restore the pending task, upload, approval pack, or onboarding draft across devices.
Contextual routes are not allowed to become a sixth permanent tab.

source_refs.rationale: Home is one of the five permanent destinations.
Home layout modules are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: The request card stays mounted with request-version posture explicit during rebase or reconnect.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the request card, current upload row, or upload affordance that initiated the transfer.
rows.artifact_posture: Transfer, scan, validation, accepted, rejected, superseded, and replacement-only states stay visibly distinct.
step_up_checkpoints: Sensitive uploads remain request-bound and may require route-local confirmation before final attachment.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Resumed uploads preserve frozen tenant, client, request, and request-version identity.
Rejected and superseded uploads remain visible as recovery context but never become default current artifacts.

source_refs.rationale: The upload phases and request-binding rules are explicit.
Upload session allocation is the sole non-command mutation surface for raw bytes.

rows.stale_view_posture: The approval pack remains visible while stale-signoff posture blocks unsafe commitment.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the digest anchor, declaration section, or the sign-off trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Current approval pack and signed receipt stay explicit; superseded packs remain historical only.
step_up_checkpoints: Step-up stays contained inside the route and must not route-switch the client into a different shell.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Return from external sign-off handoff never implies completion until the governed approval read model settles.
Historical approval packs remain available but cannot become the current default by drift.

source_refs.rationale: Approval posture, step-up, and stale protection are explicit.
Rebased approval and historical pack posture are explicit scenarios.

rows.stale_view_posture: Previously entered answers and resumable draft posture stay visible during refresh, reconnect, and rebase.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the active step header, current form field group, or support entry anchor.
rows.artifact_posture: Onboarding confirmations and route-local uploads stay subordinate to the active step workspace.
step_up_checkpoints: Identity and eligibility checks stay inside the journey step where they are relevant.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Save-and-return preserves the current step, answers, and any in-progress upload sessions.
Onboarding is only a permanent destination while active.

source_refs.rationale: Step save-and-return and support posture are explicit.
Mobile-width onboarding rules are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: The case context panel stays mounted and makes hidden/unavailable states plain-language rather than implicit.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the support entry trigger, top question row, or the originating route action that opened Help.
rows.artifact_posture: Support history and current case context stay explicit and subordinate to the active help action.
step_up_checkpoints: Escalated help flows may invoke route-local verification while preserving the case context panel.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Help requests preserve route, reason family, and context bundle when resumed.
Return targets after support handoff restore the originating route and focus anchor.

source_refs.rationale: Help route modules are explicit.
The durable `CLIENT_PORTAL_REQUEST_HELP` command is explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Overview cards stay mounted while stale basis or access drift is surfaced inline.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the overview attention summary row or pending change queue item that launched deeper work.
rows.artifact_posture: Overview is summary-first and never exports broader detail than the current filtered slice permits.
step_up_checkpoints: Risky follow-on routes may trigger inline step-up before mutation staging begins.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected object, dominant question, and filters survive responsive collapse and browser back.
Overview attention rows preserve the same route anchor across refresh.

source_refs.rationale: Overview widgets and route purpose are explicit.
The overview endpoint is explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: The staged diff remains visible but cannot commit once policy snapshot or dependency topology drifts.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the staged diff row, change basket item, or approval composer trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Diffs, blast radius, and history are first-class and remain visible beside staged change posture.
step_up_checkpoints: Approval submission and any privilege-widening change remain inline and basis-hash guarded.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Change basket retains context across refresh, reconnect, and responsive collapse.
The exact basis hash must be preserved when approving or submitting staged changes.

source_refs.rationale: Tenant config widgets and basket/approval posture are explicit.
Policy snapshot and dependency topology hashes are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Principal detail stays visible while mutation controls fail closed when access basis drifts.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the principal directory row or authority-chain invocation anchor.
rows.artifact_posture: Principal detail, authority chain, and staged access changes remain side-by-side.
step_up_checkpoints: Any privilege-widening change must preserve simulation basis and step-up posture.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Roving selection preserves the selected principal under responsive collapse.
Authority chain detail remains attached to the selected principal rather than resetting to list top.

source_refs.rationale: Principal route purpose and detail are explicit.
The principals endpoint is explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Matrix stays visible while mutation affordances fail closed on outdated basis.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected role row, matrix cell, or change basket invocation.
rows.artifact_posture: Role diffs, impact, and history remain visible before commit.
step_up_checkpoints: Role changes that widen authority preserve both topology and simulation basis hashes.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected role and visible diff row survive refresh and browser back.
Role edits preserve the current comparison anchor after re-render.

source_refs.rationale: Role route purpose follows the access map.
The role matrix endpoint is explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Simulation result remains visible but cannot be committed after basis or topology drift.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the simulation parameter set, result card, or commit trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Simulation basis, dependency topology, and potential blast radius are treated as first-class artifacts.
step_up_checkpoints: Committing a simulated change requires the exact simulation basis hash and any route-local step-up.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Simulation basis hash and dependency topology hash are preserved across receipts and retries.
The simulator cannot silently rerun under a different basis when resumed.

source_refs.rationale: The simulator route is explicit.
The simulation endpoint and basis-hash rules are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Health detail remains visible while repair actions fail closed on outdated inventory or topology basis.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected authority-link row or detail expansion trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Binding mismatch, delegation gap, and environment drift are distinct and remain inspectable as separate artifacts.
step_up_checkpoints: Repair commands stay basis-hash guarded and may demand step-up for authority-widening effects.
recovery_and_resume_rules: The selected link item remains anchored through refresh and export eligibility checks.
Repair posture cannot silently downgrade mismatch severity or merge categories after resume.

source_refs.rationale: The route semantics are explicit.
The authority-links endpoint is explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: The matrix remains visible while commit actions fail closed on drifted retention frames.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected policy row or impact preview toggle.
rows.artifact_posture: Policy matrix and impact preview remain paired; export follows the active filtered slice only.
step_up_checkpoints: Lowering retention minimums or widening erase posture remains step-up and approval guarded.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Retention focus order is normalized from the shared governance shell law plus route-local workspaces.
Selected policy row and impact preview remain attached under responsive collapse.

source_refs.rationale: Retention routes are explicit.
The retention endpoint is explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Register entries remain visible but irreversible actions fail closed on stale retention basis.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the legal-hold row or impact preview trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Hold records, policy impacts, and downstream constraints remain visible together.
step_up_checkpoints: Opening or releasing a hold requires inline step-up and approval posture when policy demands it.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Legal hold selection and impact preview remain anchored under layout collapse.
Hold state is never inferred from a policy matrix alone; the register stays authoritative.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.
The retention frame is the northbound source for legal-hold slices.

rows.stale_view_posture: Queue context remains visible while erase actions fail closed with typed stale-view errors.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the erasure queue row or blocker explanation anchor.
rows.artifact_posture: Eligibility, blocking holds, and irreversible consequences are all first-class rows, not hidden edge cases.
step_up_checkpoints: Irreversible erasure actions are never accepted from a stale governance view.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected erasure row and blocker detail stay mounted across refresh and back.
The route preserves the same filtered slice for export and receipt review.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.
Unmasked audit export and irreversible retention actions cannot commit from stale views.

rows.stale_view_posture: Investigation frames stay visible while export or follow-on actions fail closed when masking or basis drift.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected event row or export eligibility trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Append-only event slices, correlation neighborhoods, and export eligibility remain explicit and non-destructive.
step_up_checkpoints: Export remains governed by the active filtered slice and masking/export posture.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected event, diff inspector state, and export eligibility panel survive refresh and responsive collapse.
Audit slices remain append-only and never reorder into heuristic summaries.

source_refs.rationale: Audit route purpose is explicit.
The audit-investigations endpoint is explicit.

visibility_lanes: CALM operator truth after northbound reconciliation
rows.stale_view_posture: Previously valid content stays mounted while inspector actions fail closed and refresh/rebase becomes explicit.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the manifest sidebar row, canvas anchor, or inspector trigger that opened support detail.
rows.artifact_posture: Evidence preview, compare context, and pinned selections stay inside cache-safe native affordances.
step_up_checkpoints: Native step-up still delegates to governed backend/browser flows rather than inventing offline legal commands.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Scene restoration never reopens a manifest after tenant switch, privilege downgrade, or masking drift.
Resize or inspector collapse must preserve the same manifest scene identity, dominant question, and settlement posture.

source_refs.rationale: The primary split-view scene is explicit.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Native secondary windows and preview subjects
source_refs.rationale: Scene legality is cache-bound.

visibility_lanes: CALM operator truth after northbound reconciliation
rows.stale_view_posture: The scene stays mounted but any stale command lane downgrades inline to read-only or refresh-required posture.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the invoking sidebar item, selected module anchor, or inspector trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Current workspace detail may be cached locally, but mutations still respect stale-view guards from the rendered surface.
step_up_checkpoints: No blind offline legal commands; backend stale-view guards still bind every mutation-capable affordance.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Reconnect and rebase must match the browser collaboration contract.
Scene restoration keeps the same work item, dominant question, and settlement posture when still legal.

source_refs.rationale: Work-item data flow and cursor/resume model are explicit.
Rebase, stale-view, and scene-identity parity are explicit acceptance criteria.

rows.stale_view_posture: If the parent scene basis drifts, the compare window becomes read-only or closes back to the parent anchor.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the exact parent compare trigger or selected evidence row.
rows.artifact_posture: Compare windows are support-only and cannot silently widen preview scope beyond the parent subject.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Detached windows serialize as `NativeOperatorSecondaryWindowScene` with parent return metadata.
Preview subject legality is rechecked before scene restoration.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Compare windows are explicit.
Preview subject legality and temp-artifact purges are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Audit content may remain visible but any export or print action fails closed on drift.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the parent audit trigger or provenance row anchor.
rows.artifact_posture: Audit detail stays non-authoritative locally and must respect masking/export posture at open time and restore time.
step_up_checkpoints: Export or print remains governed by masking/export posture before any helper opens.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Parent focus restoration is serialized and mandatory.
Quick Look, print, and export helpers purge temp artifacts if tenant, masking, or preview subject drift.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Audit windows are explicit.
Quick Look and export legality are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Packet preview may persist visually while export controls fail closed on drift.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the parent packet review trigger or selected evidence row.
rows.artifact_posture: Print-preview and export staging are governed helpers, not independent truth surfaces.
step_up_checkpoints: Export and print helpers must recheck masking/export posture before opening or restoring.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Temp artifacts purge when tenant, masking, or preview subject drift.
The secondary window must restore back to the exact parent packet trigger.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Filing packet review/export windows are explicit.
Temporary artifact purge rules are explicit.

rows.stale_view_posture: Authority review can stay visible, but action affordances fail closed when the parent basis drifts.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the parent authority review trigger.
rows.artifact_posture: Authority review stays tied to the invoking object and cannot survive into a broader context.
step_up_checkpoints: Any authority action still routes through governed backend or browser step-up paths.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Detached authority review restores to the exact parent invocation anchor.
Route legality is rechecked before the detached window resumes.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Authority interaction review is explicit.
Authority work still uses governed browser/session flows when needed.

rows.stale_view_posture: Parent scene remains preserved behind the handoff and resumes only after legality checks and fresh read settlement.
rows.focus_return_rule: Return to the exact parent scene trigger that initiated the handoff.
rows.artifact_posture: No external handoff implies completion until the parent governed read model confirms settlement.
step_up_checkpoints: The handoff itself is the step-up checkpoint and must serialize the parent return target.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Temporary external handoff is allowed but must restore the exact parent route/scene and focus anchor.
Tenant switch, privilege downgrade, or masking drift invalidate the return envelope rather than resuming heuristically.

source_refs.rationale: ASWebAuthenticationSession is explicit.
External handoff must return to the same object and shell.
